events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need assess scenario: commercial duct installation, rough-complete, observed sagging flex >1/2 in/ft between supports. Need determine decision fail because non-compliant. Risk? medium/high. Sagging flex can restrict airflow, cause noise, reduce efficiency, but not immediate safety critical. In commercial code governed, third-party inspections. Defect: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: scenario says treat as roughly 70% complete until defects corrected. So percent_complete 70. Value not relevant 0. Sound_source etc default empty string/0. Workable empty. Findings: flex duct sag exceeds 1/2 in/ft between supports, violates IMC 603/SMACNA/Manual D support spacing/slope. Actions: add/reposition supports, replace damaged flex if any, re-inspect. Rationale. References: IMC 603, SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards, ACCA Manual D. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.label: response

  "findings": [
    "Flex duct sags more than 1/2 in/ft between supports",
    "Support spacing and sag do not comply with IMC 603 / SMACNA / Manual D requirements",
    "Sagging can reduce airflow, increase static pressure, and create noise"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Add or reposition supports so flex duct sag does not exceed 1/2 in/ft",
    "Inspect full flex duct runs for kinks, compression, or damage and replace as needed",
    "Re-check support spacing and alignment per approved submittals and standards",
    "Schedule re-inspection before closing in or final test-and-balance"
  ],
  "rationale": "The flex duct installation is roughed in but has excessive sag between supports, which is a code/standard non-compliance. Until supports are corrected and the duct is re-inspected, the installation cannot pass rough inspection and overall completion is held at roughly 70%.",
  "references": [
    "IMC 603",
    "SMACNA HVAC Duct Construction Standards",
    "ACCA Manual D"
  ]
}
